Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Criminal Code and the Corrections and Conditional Release Act (addiction treatment in penitentiaries)
Identification.RunningHead: An Act to amend the Criminal Code and the Corrections and Conditional Release Act (addiction treatment in penitentiaries)

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Criminal Code to provide that, on request by a person who is sentenced to imprisonment in a penitentiary, a court may, if certain conditions are met, make a recommendation that the person serve their sentence, or a part of it, in custody in a penitentiary that has been designated as an addiction treatment facility.

The enactment also amends the Corrections and Conditional Release Act to, among other things, provide for the designation of a penitentiary as an addiction treatment facility and require the Correctional Service of Canada to ensure that a person in respect of whom a recommendation is made that they serve their sentence, or a part of it, in a designated facility is placed in such a facility as soon as reasonably possible.
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: The Criminal Code is amended by adding the following after section 743.1:

Section.MarginalNote: Sentence to be served in designated facility

Subsection.Text: On request by a person who is sentenced to imprisonment in a penitentiary, a court may make a recommendation that the person serve their sentence, or a part of it, in custody in a designated facility as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Corrections and Conditional Release Act if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: there is evidence establishing a pattern of repetitive behaviour by the offender that indicates that problematic substance use has contributed to the offenderâs involvement in the criminal justice system;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the offender consents to participating in the treatment program provided at the facility;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the court is satisfied that the fact that the offender serves their sentence, or a part of it, in a designated facility would be consistent with the fundamental purpose and principles of sentencing set out in sections 718 to 718.201;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the offence of which the individual is convicted is not an offence, prosecuted by way of indictment, for which the maximum term of imprisonment is 14 years or life; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the offence of which the individual is convicted is not an offence, prosecuted by way of indictment, for which the maximum term of imprisonment is 10 years, that

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: involved the import, export, trafficking or production of drugs, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: involved the use of a weapon.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Report to court by Correctional Service
Subsection.Text: A court that makes a recommendation under subsection (1) shall forward to the Correctional Service of Canada its reasons for the recommendation, any relevant reports that were submitted to it in relation to the recommendation, and any other information relevant to administering the sentence or committal.
Paragraph (a) of the definition penitentiary in subsection 2(1) of the Corrections and Conditional Release Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a facility of any description â including a designated facility â together with all lands connected with it, that is operated, permanently or temporarily, by the Service for the care and custody of inmates, and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 2(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: designated facilityâmeans a facility designated under subsection 30.1(1); (Ã©tablissement dÃ©signÃ©)

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 15.1(1)(b)(ii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: their participation in programs, including programs in a designated facility, and

Subsection.Text: Section 15.1 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

In developing the plan, the institutional head shall take into account any information relating to the offenderâs substance use that was forwarded to them under subsection 743.11(2) of the Criminal Code.
Subsection 15.1(2.01) of the Act is replaced by the following:

In order to ensure that the plan can be developed in a manner that takes any mental health needs of the offender into consideration, including any that relate to substance use, the institutional head shall, as soon as practicable after the day on which the offender is received but not later than the 30th day after that day, refer the offenderâs case to the portion of the Service that administers health care for the purpose of conducting a mental health assessment of the offender.

Section.Text: Paragraph 28(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the availability of appropriate programs and services, including programs and services that are provided in a designated facility, and the personâs willingness to participate in those programs.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 28:

Section.MarginalNote: Sentence to be served in designated facility
Section.Text: If a recommendation is made under subsection 743.11(1) of the Criminal Code that a person who is or is to be confined in a penitentiary serve their sentence, or a part of it, in a designated facility, the Service shall ensure that the person is placed in such a facility as soon as reasonably possible.
Section 29 of the Act is amended by striking out âorâ at the end of paragraph (b) and by adding the following after that paragraph:

Paragraph.Label: (b.1)
Paragraph.Text: within a penitentiary, from an area that has been assigned a security classification under section 29.1 to a designated facility within the same or another penitentiary, in accordance with the regulations made under paragraph 96(f.1); or

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 30:

Subsection.Text: The Commissioner may designate a penitentiary or any area in a penitentiary to be an addiction treatment facility.

The purpose of an addiction treatment facility is to provide an inmate with access to a program for curative treatment in relation to their problematic substance use as well as to other related services that respond to their specific needs.

The Minister, or a person authorized by the Minister, may enter into an agreement with a province for the provision of addiction treatment programs to offenders in a designated facility and for payment by the Minister, or by a person authorized by the Minister, in respect of the provision of those programs.

Section.Text: The definition health care in section 85 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: health careâmeans medical care, dental care and mental health care, provided by registered health care professionals or by persons acting under the supervision of registered health care professionals, and includes care that is provided as part of an addiction treatment program in a designated facility; (soins de santÃ©)

Section.Text: Section 96 of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (f):

Paragraph.Label: (f.1)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the confinement of inmates in a designated facility, including respecting the making of a determination by an institutional head or the Commissioner, in consultation with addiction treatment experts, as to whether the treatment provided to an inmate in a designated facility should be altered or as to whether an inmate should remain in such a facility;
